fixed_gaps.description: No central ENV_BLOCKLIST utility existed — created with 31 hardcoded secret env var names + 5 suffix patterns (_TOKEN, _KEY, _SECRET, _PASSWORD, _CREDENTIAL)
sanitizedProcessEnv() was a no-op that copied ALL process.env unfiltered
Sidecar subprocess (createSidecarEnv) passed ALL process.env to the sidecar child process unfiltered
Cross-spawn-spawner (subprocess factory) extended parent env unfiltered
OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT not in any blocklist, leaking serialized auth credentials to all subprocesses

remaining_gaps.description: NO general-purpose output scrubber — tool output (bash stdout/stderr, file contents, grep results) is never scanned for secrets before returning to LLM context, logging to disk, or persisting to session storage
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Create a SecretScanner utility that regex-scans tool output at the boundary. Reuse patterns from debug-packet redactValues() (packages/opencode/src/debug/debug-packet.ts:157) which already covers sk-or-, bearer tokens, AKIA/ASIA keys, and private key PEM blocks. Apply before returning to agent context and before persisting to session storage.

remaining_gaps.description: Bash tool (smart_bash) uses spawnSync with shell:true and passes NO env option — inherits FULL process.env including all credentials injected by provider.ts. Any bash command can access and exfiltrate API keys, tokens, and auth credentials via 'echo $VAR' or 'env'.
affected_code: packages/opencode/src/tool/smart-bash.ts (line 79) — spawnSync(cmd, [], { shell: true }) with no env
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Pass sanitizedProcessEnv() as the env option. Also add dangerous command pattern detection beyond 6 DESTRUCTIVE strings.

remaining_gaps.description: OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT bypasses ENV_BLOCKLIST via explicit opts.env override — workspace.ts:571 serializes full auth state (all OAuth tokens, API keys) into JSON string placed in process.env for workspace adapter subprocess. The explicit opts.env assignment takes priority over sanitized defaults.
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Refactor credential passing to Effect Config. Short term: limit workspace adapter env to only the specific credential needed, not full auth.all() dump.

remaining_gaps.description: Provider layer injects credentials directly into global process.env — AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK and AICORE_SERVICE_KEY written to process.env for SDK consumption. These injected credentials then leak to EVERY child process that inherits process.env.
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: This is the ROOT CAUSE of env leaks to child processes. Provider SDKs that require env vars should receive them via per-spawn env overrides, not global process.env injection. Add these 3 env var names to ENV_BLOCKLIST, then refactor to pass them explicitly only to the specific subprocesses that need them.

remaining_gaps.description: PTY terminal inherits FULL process.env with no sanitization — pty/index.ts:199 spreads ...process.env into child process env
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Apply sanitizeEnv() before spreading process.env into PTY child env.

remaining_gaps.description: MCP StdioClientTransport receives FULL process.env — mcp/index.ts:456 spreads ...process.env, bypassing CrossSpawnSpawner's sanitization
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Apply sanitizeEnv() to the env spread passed to StdioClientTransport.

remaining_gaps.description: LSP language server spawns inherit FULL process.env — lsp/lsp.ts:180 spreads ...process.env
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Apply sanitizeEnv() before spreading process.env into LSP child env.

remaining_gaps.description: Other tool subprocess spawns (smart-git, smart-grep, smart-bun, project-command, binary/manager, DuckDB, desktop git IPC) all inherit full process.env with no sanitization
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Add sanitizedProcessEnv() as the env option for all spawnSync/spawn/exec calls.

remaining_gaps.description: Smart bash tool logs full command text to analytics (bash_usage.v1.jsonl) — includes raw command parameter which could contain inlined secrets, env vars, or tokens
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Strip sensitive env var references from logged command text.

remaining_gaps.description: Enterprise storage reads AWS secrets directly from process.env — OPENCODE_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY not runtime-protected
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Wrap in Effect Config.redacted() pattern like OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL.

remaining_gaps.description: No dangerous command pattern detection beyond 6 DESTRUCTIVE patterns — no command substitution detection $(...), backtick blocking, env var injection scanning, or backslash-escaped path detection
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Add detect_dangerous_bash_patterns() matching the Rig Relay spec in AGENTS.md.

remaining_gaps.description: No bash command rerouting in source-level smart-bash.ts — only userland plugin (.opencode/tools/smart_bash.ts:68-99) implements cat→read_source, grep→smart_grep mapping
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Port rerouting table from userland plugin to source-level smart_bash tool.

remaining_gaps.description: Auth credentials persisted on disk in auth.json (0o600) with no OS keychain integration — all OAuth tokens and API keys stored as plaintext JSON. Only GitHub token uses safeStorage encryption.
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Audit data directory permissions. Consider OS keychain integration. Use safeStorage encryption pattern from github-ipc.ts:56 for all credential stores.

remaining_gaps.description: OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD NOT in ENV_BLOCKLIST — passes through sanitizeEnv to all child processes. Also directly passed to sidecar via sidecar.ts:95.
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Add OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD to ENV_BLOCKLIST. For sidecar, pass via a separate channel or narrow env override.

remaining_gaps.description: OPENCODE_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID and OPENCODE_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY NOT in ENV_BLOCKLIST — enterprise storage credentials leak to all child processes
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Add both to ENV_BLOCKLIST. Use Effect Config.redacted() pattern for credential loading.

remaining_gaps.description: EXA_API_KEY embedded in module-constant URL at import time — stored in memory as 'https://mcp.exa.ai/mcp?exaApiKey=...'. Could leak via error messages, debug output, or process inspection.
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Construct URL lazily at call time. Move key out of module-level constant.

remaining_gaps.description: MCP OAuth tokens stored in plaintext mcp-auth.json (0o600 only) — includes access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, and code verifiers
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Encrypt MCP tokens using same pattern as GitHub token (safeStorage) or OS keychain.

remaining_gaps.description: Console cloud functions log full request/response bodies to stdout — Stripe webhook body (with PII), Honeycomb webhook payload, Salesforce OAuth response body (may contain tokens), OAuth response objects
remaining_gaps.recommended_fix: Remove or redact all console.log calls that log full HTTP bodies. Use structured logging with automatic PII redaction.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: HTTP recorder redaction
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: HTTP recording/replay only — redacts URL query params, headers, credential patterns (sk-or-, AKIA, private keys)
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Only runs during HTTP recording; does not cover live tool output

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: LLM error diagnostics redaction
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: LLM provider error messages only — redacts headers, URL query params, JSON body fields
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Only runs on provider errors; does not cover agent-facing tool output

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Debug packet config redaction (two-pass: key-name + value scanning)
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Debug export only — key-name patterns (apiKey, token, secret, password, credential) + value regex scanning (sk-or-, bearer, AKIA, private keys)
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Not applied to live output streams. Known weakness: case-sensitive key matching (ApiKey not caught). Value scanning depth limit 5.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Plugin config secret stripping
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Plugin config only — strips apiKey, token, password, secret, key, credential, auth from config before plugins without secrets.read
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Does not cover tool output. 'key' over-broad may match benign keys. Missing patterns for url, connection, database keys.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Database connection string redaction
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Postgres error messages only — replaces PG connection strings with [REDACTED]

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Session export sanitization
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Session export only — strips permissions, share URLs, paths from exported session metadata
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Only strips structural metadata, not message content secrets. Full message history exported raw.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Flight recorder event prefix filter
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: In-memory ring buffer — allowlist filters excluded event types (llm.*, session.message.*, file.*)
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Allowlist-based; any new sensitive event type not in exclude list would be recorded.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: Effect Redacted type
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Provider API keys, database URL, server password — wrapped in Redacted which prevents console.log/JSON.stringify exposure
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Only used in 3 narrow contexts. Most credentials use raw strings in process.env.

existing_narrow_scrubbers.name: DuckDB SQL firewall
existing_narrow_scrubbers.scope: Blocks 10 dangerous DuckDB functions + COPY...TO — prevents file-read via SQL injection
existing_narrow_scrubbers.gap: Does not prevent reading database content that may contain stored secrets. Known false-positive substring matching bug.

output_scrubbing_status.missing: No mechanism scans tool output (bash stdout/stderr, file contents returned by tools, agent message text) for secrets before returning to LLM context or persisting to session storage. Also: file logger (packages/core/src/util/log.ts:92) writes arbitrary extra fields to disk without redaction. Effect logger (packages/core/src/effect/logger.ts:38) forwards fiber annotations to OTLP/file without filtering. Config parse errors logged with raw strings that may contain config content. Client-controlled log handler (control.ts:27) accepts arbitrary messages without validation.

details.finding: smart_bash (source) spawns with shell:true, no env option — inherits full process.env including injected credentials
smart_bash (userland plugin) spawns with shell:true, no env option — same gap, same severity
Provider layer injects credentials into global process.env (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN) — this is the root cause of bash env leaks
PTY inherits full process.env with no sanitization
MCP StdioClientTransport receives full process.env, bypassing CrossSpawnSpawner sanitization
LSP language server spawns inherit full process.env
Smart tools (git, grep, bun, project-command) spawn without env filtering
Binary manager spawns without env filtering
DuckDB subprocess spawns without env filtering
Desktop git IPC uses exec without env filtering
Only 6 DESTRUCTIVE patterns detected — no command substitution detection, backtick blocking, env var injection scanning
Source-level smart-bash.ts has NO rerouting (userland plugin does: cat→read_source, grep→smart_grep, etc.)

fixed_sites: CrossSpawnSpawner (cross-spawn-spawner.ts:109) — sanitizeEnv applied when extendEnv: true. Covers worktree, MCP pgrep.

auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Full auth state (OAuth tokens, API keys, well-known credentials) serialized to JSON string via JSON.stringify(yield* auth.all()). Explicit opts.env override bypasses ENV_BLOCKLIST for the workspace adapter, but the env var is readable by any code in the adapter process.
Auth.all() checks process.env.OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT and JSON.parses it. Any code in the workspace adapter process can read this env var.
Account module also reads OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT from process.env directly. Third consumer of the serialized credential blob.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: Provider credential injection into global process.env
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, and OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN injected directly into process.env for SDK consumption. These persist for the entire process lifetime and leak to all child processes that inherit process.env.
OPENCODE_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY read directly from process.env and passed to S3 client. No Redacted wrapper.
All auth credentials persisted to auth.json in Global.Path.data with file mode 0o600.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: Bash tool inherits full process.env (including injected credentials)
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Every bash command inherits ALL credentials injected into process.env. Commands like 'env', 'echo $AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK' expose secrets in output returned to agent context.
PTY sessions can access all credentials in process.env. User typing 'env' in terminal sees all secrets.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: MCP and LSP subprocesses inherit full process.env
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: External tool processes can read all credentials from their inherited environment.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: Effect logger and file logger write without redaction
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Both loggers write arbitrary extra fields and fiber annotations without secret filtering. If auth data accidentally enters log context, it's written to disk in plaintext.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD passed to sidecar child process
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Server password explicitly set in opts.env for sidecar, bypassing any blocklist. Any code in the sidecar process can read it from process.env.
API key embedded in a URL string at module initialization time. The URL with key persists in memory for the process lifetime. Could leak via error messages, debug output, or heap inspection.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: MCP OAuth tokens in plaintext mcp-auth.json
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, and code verifiers stored as plaintext JSON. Only file permissions (0o600) protect them.
Uses safeStorage.encryptString() + base64 for GitHub token — the ONLY encrypted-at-rest credential store in the codebase. All other stores are plaintext.

auth_token_exposure_points.point: Console cloud functions log full HTTP bodies
auth_token_exposure_points.exposure: Multiple cloud functions log full request/response bodies to stdout (Stripe webhook with PII, Honeycomb payloads, Salesforce OAuth responses with tokens). Production logging leak vectors.

summary.key_insight: RL-004 Layer 2 (ENV_BLOCKLIST + sanitizeEnv) was correctly implemented at the subprocess infrastructure layer (cross-spawn-spawner, ripgrep, sidecar). However, the tool execution path, PTY, MCP, and LSP layers were NOT covered — all still inherit full process.env. Additionally, the ROOT CAUSE was missed: provider.ts injects credentials (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AICORE_SERVICE_KEY) directly into global process.env for SDK consumption. These injected credentials leak to every child process. Several env vars are also missing from the blocklist: OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD, OPENCODE_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID, OPENCODE_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.
summary.assessment: The highest-impact next steps, in priority order: (1) stop provider credential injection into global process.env (refactor to per-spawn env overrides), (2) add sanitizedProcessEnv to all tool/bash/PTY/MCP/LSP spawns, (3) add missing env vars (OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD, OPENCODE_STORAGE_*) to ENV_BLOCKLIST, (4) build a SecretScanner for tool output at the agent boundary.
